_for_consumption_by: Module 6 — Defining Interfaces (N2 chart, DFDs, sequence diagrams)

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: Founder Web UI
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Intake conversation, spec review, quick-start trigger

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: Session Manager
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Holds per-founder mode choice + auth; chooses routing policy

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: LLM Provider Layer
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Routing across Anthropic / OpenAI / local model endpoints

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: Prompt Cache
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Claude prompt-caching layer + local embedding cache

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: CLI Bundle Emitter
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Packaging + signing + delivery of CLI bundle

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: Probe SDK (Customer-side)
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Runs inside customer system; emits metrics

candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.name: Recommendation Engine
candidate_subsystems_for_n2_rows_and_col.role: Consumes metric deviations + produces cited recommendations

top_imputed_importance_interfaces.ec: EC8 Context tokens per agent call
top_imputed_importance_interfaces.interface_anchor: SS3 Agent Orchestrator → SS4 LLM Provider Layer
SS9 Probe SDK → SS10 Metrics Collector
SS6 Spec Generator → SS7 Traceback Store
SS2 Session Manager → SS4 LLM Provider Layer

cross_interface_coordination_constraints.constraint: Probe SDK ↔ Collector contract must expose joint tuning
cross_interface_coordination_constraints.n2_chart_note: probe_frequency and aggregation_window flow together; N2 cell (SS9,SS10) must carry both as joint SLAs.

cross_interface_coordination_constraints.constraint: Traceback cache TTL and vendor-doc refresh cadence must invalidate together
cross_interface_coordination_constraints.n2_chart_note: Single subsystem SS7 owns both; interface is intra-subsystem but sequence diagram must show the invalidation event.

cross_interface_coordination_constraints.constraint: Routing policy decision must precede parallel dispatch; cannot dispatch before session mode is locked
cross_interface_coordination_constraints.n2_chart_note: Synchronous dependency — sequence diagram shows SS2.lock_mode() before SS3.dispatch_agents().

cross_interface_coordination_constraints.constraint: Batch size and aggregation window must not starve each other (batch may never fill within window)
cross_interface_coordination_constraints.n2_chart_note: Add time-based flush trigger to SS9→SS10 contract as fallback when batch doesn't fill.

external._definition: Interfaces that cross the c1v <-> customer boundary; regulated by customer contract.

operational._definition: Interfaces between c1v-internal subsystems that carry user-facing performance SLAs.

internal._definition: Interfaces that are purely implementation details but carry design-target constraints.

open_questions_for_module_6: Which sequence diagrams most concretely show the roof tradeoffs — specifically the SS7 internal cache/fetcher interaction (EC9↔EC11)?

Should SS5 Prompt Cache be a separate subsystem or absorbed into SS3 Agent Orchestrator for the N2 chart? M5 treats it as separate because EC7 has high leverage, but it may simplify to one cell in M6.
Does SS9 Probe SDK belong in the N2 chart at all (it lives on customer infrastructure) or does it count as an external actor treated like SS-External? Affects N2 row/col count.

How should the P9 sensitivity mitigation (pin SOTA Cloud as default) surface in the interface set? Proposed: SS2 Session Manager owns the default-mode invariant and exposes it via a single interface that SS3 consumes.
How to represent the dual-mode fork (Cloud vs Local) in a sequence diagram without duplicating every interaction? Candidate: parameterized lifeline on SS4 (LLM Provider Layer).
